case_metadata.title: Phishing → Browser Download → Execution → Exfiltration
case_metadata.ground_truth_provenance: Authored alongside the bundled browser history, downloads database, and network artefacts in the disk/Users/analyst fixture.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Phishing URL with suspicious TLD (.tk) accessed via Outlook referrer
ground_truth_findings.rationale: URL `finance-update-secure.tk` with referrer from `outlook.office.com/mail` = phishing email entry vector confirmed.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Raw IPv4 + non-standard port URL (203.0.113.42:8080) accessed via redirect
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Raw IP + non-443 port indicates direct payload delivery, not standard HTTPS.
Double-extension trick (.pdf.exe) — classic phishing delivery signature.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Download → execution chain correlated within 6-minute window
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Browser download record timestamps line up with Amcache/Prefetch execution by 6 min.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Compressed archive uploaded to bashupload.com / transfer.sh
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Known file-drop services in browser history during attack window = data leaving the host.

ground_truth_findings.claim: Initial H1 'OneDriveStartup persistence' retracted after parse_registry_hive shows legitimate Microsoft path
ground_truth_findings.rationale: Hero hallucination-management demonstration: agent retracts a wrong claim, identifies the true HKCU\...\Run\WinUpdate as actual persistence. Confidence 0.62 → 0.91.
